LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PHILLIP SANCHEZ AND J. VALLES TRUCKING, INC. MOTION TO DETERMINE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 877.6. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED, SETTLEMENT IS IN THE BALLPARK. COURT GRANTS JOINDER. THERE IS NO DISPUTE THAT HARRIS SALINAS REBAR INC. IS AN ADDITIONAL INSURED UNDER MOVANT'S POLICY AND THEREFORE SETTLEMENT IS IN GOOD FAITH AS TO BOTH SETTLING DEFENDANTS. THIS ORDER DOES NOT AFFECT CLAIMS FOR EXPRESS INDEMNITY. THE COURT ADDS: THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION ON THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DUTY TO DEFEND REMAINS, OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DISMISSED. (COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KATY ANNE BERSAMIN, CSR #11722

Notice And Joinder Of Harris Salinas Rebar, Inc. In Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS HARRIS SALINAS REBAR, INC. Notice and joinder of Harris Salinas Rebar, Inc. in Motion for Determination of Good Faith Settlement. Appearances: Daniel J. Kelly, Esq. Haiaght Brown & Bonesteel LLP (415) 546-7500 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Michael A. Walker, Esq. of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz (415) 433-0990 appearing for CC Myers, Inc. and The State of California. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted, settlement is in the ballpark. Court grants joinder. There is no dispute that Harris Salinas Rebar Inc. is an additional insured under movant's policy and therefore settlement is in good faith as to both settling defendants. This order does not affect claims for express indemnity. The Court adds: The seventh cause of action on the cross-complaint duty to defend remains, other causes of action are dismissed. (Counsel to meet and confer regarding a proposed Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722

Motion To Determine Good Faith Of Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 877.6;
Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PHILLIP SANCHEZ, and, J. VALLES TRUCKING, INC. Motion to Determine Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To CCP 877.6. Appearances: Greg Schaffer, Esq. of Low Ball and Lynch (415) 981-6630 appearing for Defendants and Moving party; Michael A. Walker, Esq. of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz (415) 433-0990 appearing for CC Myers, Inc. and The State of California. (The Court makes disclosure on the record.) Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted, settlement is in the ballpark. Court grants joinder. There is no dispute that Harris Salinas Rebar Inc. is an additional insured under movant's policy and therefore settlement is in good faith as to both settling defendants. This order does not affect claims for express indemnity. The Court adds: The seventh cause of action on the cross-complaint duty to defend remains, other causes of action are dismissed. (Counsel to meet and confer regarding a proposed Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722
